title: AI Dev Team Gate
description: Base contract for every pipeline gate JSON written under pipeline/gates/. Per-stage schemas extend this with stage-specific required fields. Field semantics are defined in .devteam/rules/gates-core.md (universal) and each stage's rules/stage-NN.md; core/gates/validator.js is the authoritative reader. A gate may be a workstream gate (per-role, written by a single adapter) or a stage gate (merged from workstream gates by the orchestrator).

stage.description: Stage identifier matching the gate's filename, e.g. 'stage-04' or 'stage-05-backend'.

status.description: Outcome of the stage or workstream. ESCALATE halts the pipeline regardless of any newer gate; see .devteam/rules/escalation.md. Stage-level merged gates inherit FAIL/ESCALATE if any workstream is FAIL/ESCALATE.

orchestrator.description: Identifier of the orchestrator that minted this gate, including version (e.g. 'devteam@1.0'). Replaces the legacy 'agent' field.

host.description: Adapter that produced this workstream (e.g. 'claude-code', 'codex'). REQUIRED on workstream gates, ABSENT on stage-level merged gates.

workstream.description: Role this workstream gate belongs to (e.g. 'backend', 'pm'). REQUIRED on workstream gates, ABSENT on stage-level merged gates.

workstreams.description: On stage-level merged gates: list of workstream entries that contributed. Each entry mirrors the workstream gate's required fields plus its specific status. ABSENT on workstream gates.

track.description: Pipeline track the gate belongs to. Tracks differ on which stages run and how many approvals each gate requires.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the gate was written. Used for the bypassed-escalation sweep.

blockers.description: Reasons the stage/workstream cannot proceed. A non-empty array typically pairs with status FAIL or ESCALATE.

warnings.description: Non-blocking concerns surfaced for visibility. Do not change the gate status.

retry_number.description: Zero-based retry counter. When >= 1, this_attempt_differs_by must be a non-empty string (enforced by validator).

previous_failure_reason.description: Short summary of why the previous attempt failed; informational only.

this_attempt_differs_by.description: What changed since the previous failure. Required when retry_number >= 1; same content twice escalates instead of retrying.

tokens_in.description: Optional. Tokens consumed by the model's input context (prompt + history) for this dispatch. Populated by the adapter when the host CLI reports it (e.g. claude --print --output-format json). Used by D6 cost telemetry.

tokens_out.description: Optional. Tokens generated by the model for this dispatch. Populated by the adapter when the host CLI reports it. Used by D6 cost telemetry.

cost_usd.description: Optional. Computed cost in USD = tokens_in × $/Mtok_in + tokens_out × $/Mtok_out. Adapter or post-hoc tool fills this; core/pricing.js holds the pricing table. Treat as estimate, not invoice.

model.description: Optional. Specific model id used by the host for this dispatch (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7', 'gpt-5', 'gemini-2.5-pro'). Distinct from host (which is the framework adapter, e.g. 'claude-code'). Required for cost_usd to be computable.

duration_ms.description: Optional. Wall-clock time the dispatch took, in milliseconds. Headless mode stamps this from runHeadless's measured wall-clock; user-driven gates can be filled in by the agent if it tracks duration. Used by D6 cost telemetry and D4 performance scoring.

model_version.description: Optional (C4 reproducibility). The vendor's exact model-version string, more specific than `model`. Example: `model` = 'claude-opus-4-7', `model_version` = 'claude-opus-4-7-20251104'. Pinning at this granularity is what makes a run reproducible across vendor serving updates.

temperature.description: Optional (C4 reproducibility). Sampling temperature used for this dispatch. 0 = deterministic-ish; higher = more variation.

seed.description: Optional (C4 reproducibility). RNG seed when the host supports seeded sampling (Anthropic API and others do at temperature 0). Not all hosts honor this; recording it is still useful for audit even when the runtime ignored it.

max_tokens.description: Optional (C4 reproducibility). max_tokens parameter passed to the model. Doesn't change determinism but bounds the search space; auditable.

system_prompt_hash.description: Optional (C4 reproducibility). SHA-256 of the rendered system prompt the model received, normalized (trailing whitespace stripped per line). Stamped by the orchestrator when it renders the prompt — the agent doesn't compute its own. Hash drift between two runs of the same stage means the prompt changed (role brief edit, skill update, rule change).

tools_hash.description: Optional (C4 reproducibility). SHA-256 of the sorted, deduplicated list of tool names available to the model. Drift means the tool surface changed — relevant for replay because the same prompt with a different toolset may produce different decisions.

dispatched_tool_budget.description: Optional (G10 tool budgets). Tool names the orchestrator declared available to this role at dispatch time, drawn from ROLE_FRONTMATTER (claude-code) or equivalent adapter.toolBudgetFor(). null means no budget was declared (full host surface). Stamped by the orchestrator for headless runs; auto-injected by the gate validator for user-driven runs. Present on workstream gates; absent on merged stage gates.

chain.description: Optional (C6 tamper-evident chain). Stamped by the orchestrator onto stage-level gates: a hash of the predecessor stage gate, so mutating an earlier gate breaks the chain. Verified by `devteam verify-chain`. Stamped automatically by mergeWorkstreamGates (multi-role) and runStageHeadless (single-role); see core/gates/chain.js.

prev_stage.description: Stage id of the predecessor gate this gate commits to (e.g. 'stage-04'), or null for the genesis (first) stage.

prev_hash.description: Canonical-JSON SHA-256 of the predecessor stage gate's full content (including its own `chain` field — the chain is transitive). null for the genesis stage.

algo.description: Hashing scheme, e.g. 'sha256-canonical-json' (sorted-key JSON serialization hashed with SHA-256).

resolved_by.description: Optional (C6 / Phase 2). Authority provenance written by `devteam run --auto-rule` when it autonomously resolves an escalation on this gate: which grant was exercised, the ruling applied, and when. Lives on the gate (not just run-log.jsonl) so it inherits the tamper-evident chain.

authority.description: The authority exercised, e.g. 'auto-rule:formatting-only' — the pre-authorized grant class the operator passed to `--auto-rule`.

grant_class.description: The Principal ruling's [class:] that matched the grant.

ruling.description: The decision text the Principal issued.

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the autonomous resolution was applied.
